title: A Death-Blow Is A Life-Blow To Some
content:
A death-blow is a life-blow to some
    Who, till they died, did not alive become;
    Who, had they lived, had died, but when
    They died, vitality begun.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Death-Blow Is A Life-Blow To Some"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
